stories.body: The U.S. military said it hit Iranian missile, drone and coastal radar facilities around the Strait of Hormuz after Tehran’s drones attacked the Singapore-flagged M/V Ever Lovely, prompting President Donald Trump to call the strike a response to “a foolish violation” of the truce and Vice-President JD Vance to warn that disagreements should be handled by phone, while Iran’s Revolutionary Guard said it fired back at U.S. positions and vowed a broader response if attacks continue.
stories.headline: U.S. strikes Iranian radar and drone sites after cargo ship attack

stories.body: Twin magnitude 7.2 and 7.5 earthquakes devastated northern Venezuela, killing at least 920 people, injuring more than 3,000, overwhelming hospitals and leaving over 50,000 missing as rescuers, including newly arrived international teams, scramble through collapsed buildings in La Guaira and Caracas while survivors decry slow official aid and rely on bare-handed searches for trapped relatives.
stories.headline: Venezuela reels after twin earthquakes kill hundreds and leave tens of thousands missing

stories.body: June temperatures surpassed national records in Germany, Belgium, the Netherlands and the UK, with Saarbrücken hitting 41.3C and Suffolk 37.3C, prompting France to postpone Paris Pride, the Solidays festival and even shut Beznau’s nuclear reactors as meteorologists warn the continent—warming twice as fast as the global average—faces mounting health, transport and energy disruptions from the extreme heat.
stories.headline: Record-breaking European heatwave forces event cancellations and strains infrastructure

stories.body: NASA said five astronauts entered their Crew Dragon safe haven for about two hours after air leakage in Russia’s Zvezda module doubled to roughly two pounds per day, but the agency allowed them back onboard once Roscosmos paused cutting into the hull, with both sides pledging a collaborative fix as the station—never evacuated in 27 years—continues to host seven crew members.
stories.headline: ISS crews briefly shelter amid Russian air leak before NASA rescinds evacuation order

stories.body: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi told Al Mayadeen that any U.S.-Iran interim deal must include an Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire and an Israeli pullout from southern Lebanon, while Tehran advisers warned Israel against striking Beirut and reaffirmed support for Hezbollah’s recent attacks as negotiations over reopening the Strait of Hormuz and easing sanctions remain entangled with parallel conflicts across the region.
stories.headline: Iran links broader peace deal to Israeli withdrawal from Lebanon while backing Hezbollah

stories.body: In a 52-47 party-line vote, Senate Republicans approved an extra $70 billion for immigration enforcement over three years but rejected attempts to permanently block President Donald Trump’s $1.8 billion “anti-weaponization” fund, prompting Democratic leader Chuck Schumer to label it a slush fund and highlighting GOP infighting as some senators sought amendments to redirect or end the controversial program before the House takes up the bill next week.
stories.headline: Senate passes $70 billion ICE boost while leaving Trump’s “anti-weaponization” fund intact

stories.body: The Republican-controlled Texas State Board of Education voted 9-5 to make excerpts from the New Testament required reading for more than five million public school students starting in 2030, pairing them with works like Dickens’s “Great Expectations” and sparking protests from interfaith groups who say the unprecedented statewide mandate is the latest step after last year’s Ten Commandments law to inject Christian teachings into classrooms.
stories.headline: Texas mandates Bible stories in statewide reading list, escalating church-state fight

stories.body: A 6-3 Supreme Court ruling lifted injunctions against the Trump administration’s plan to terminate Temporary Protected Status for roughly 350,000 Haitians and 6,000 Syrians, clearing the way for DHS to revoke work authorization for long-settled families and signaling potential future removals for nearly 1.3 million TPS holders from 17 countries who now worry they could soon be detained or deported despite decades in the United States.
stories.headline: Supreme Court greenlights end of TPS for Haitians and Syrians, raising deportation fears

stories.body: New York’s Rent Guidelines Board voted 7-1 to freeze rents on roughly one million stabilized apartments for one- and two-year leases, delivering on Mayor Zohran Mamdani’s campaign promise amid soaring housing costs even as landlord representatives resigned in protest and critics warned the freeze could strain repairs on the 41% of units covered while the administration pledges to build more housing to offset market pressures.
stories.headline: New York City freezes rents on one million regulated apartments

stories.body: Former Transportation Secretary Pete Buttigieg said Michigan child protective services temporarily placed his four-year-old twins with grandparents after an anonymous caller falsely accused him of violent crimes, forcing a 24-hour separation and forensic interviews before state police determined the allegation was fabricated, illustrating how weaponized CPS complaints have joined swatting and other harassment tactics aimed at political figures.
stories.headline: Pete Buttigieg targeted by false CPS abuse report amid surge in political swatting

stories.body: Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez meets regional leaders Saturday as the PSOE’s federal committee grapples with the 24-year prison sentence for former organizer José Luis Ábalos, the lingering imprisonment of Santos Cerdán and leaked misogynistic audio, while coalition partners openly criticize the government over corruption debates that have left Sánchez increasingly isolated in Congress and facing calls for new elections.
stories.headline: PSOE federal committee convenes amid corruption fallout and dwindling parliamentary allies

stories.body: Former Prime Minister José Luis Rodríguez Zapatero asked Judge José Luis Calama to create a separate case file to scrub private messages and calendar entries that leaked from the Plus Ultra investigation, citing privacy concerns, while the magistrate invited Spain’s tax agency to join a related probe into €1.3 million in jewels found in Zapatero’s office amid allegations of influence peddling, money laundering and tax offenses.
stories.headline: Zapatero challenges Plus Ultra judge over leaked agenda as probe expands to jewels and taxes

stories.body: PP leader Alberto Núñez Feijóo is urging his party to move past the 2017 Catalan secession crisis and cooperate tactically with Junts, a stark shift from organizing mass protests against amnesty just two years ago, but Catalan conservatives remain wary as the national leadership courts cooperation from Carles Puigdemont’s bloc to shore up parliamentary arithmetic.
stories.headline: Feijóo deepens pragmatic outreach to Junts despite Catalan party unease

stories.body: Former UCO chiefs Rafael Yuste and Alfonso López Malo told National Court Judge Santiago Pedraz that senior commanders instructed them in 2024 to “stand aside” on politically sensitive investigations, including probes into the prime minister’s brother, and denied Director Mercedes González ever disclosed her meetings with alleged fixer Leire Díez, undermining González’s Senate testimony and intensifying scrutiny of efforts to quash cases affecting the government.
stories.headline: Guardia Civil generals contradict director over alleged orders to ease off political probes

stories.body: Barcelona’s municipal social workers say they served more than 92,000 people last year but now face empty advance funds, delayed housing subsidies and overcrowded emergency shelters, forcing staff to dip into their own pockets while families with children are told to find informal room rentals despite the city spending 9.3 million euros on housing aid in 2025.
stories.headline: Barcelona social services buckle under housing emergency as aid delays mount

stories.body: Barcelona’s government ordered an urgent inspection of the Centre de la Vila mall’s underground car park after ERC documented leaks, debris and possible structural risks, warning state-owned Mercasa it could face sanctions and even a joint lawsuit with nearby residents unless it produces updated safety certificates and resolves a long-running maintenance dispute that has contributed to the complex’s commercial decline and failed auctions.
stories.headline: City threatens sanctions over neglected Centre de la Vila garage owned by state firm

stories.body: Catalan firefighters deployed 135 crew members, 11 aircraft and 50 vehicles to control a multi-front forest fire between Tiana and Montgat that forced authorities to confine about 16,000 residents across four municipalities, evacuate summer camps and issue Es-Alert warnings before stabilizing the 48-hectare blaze, while Mossos d’Esquadra arrested a suspect on suspicion of intentionally setting the simultaneous ignition points.
stories.headline: Suspected arsonist detained after wildfire near Tiana burns 48 hectares and triggers mass confinements

stories.body: The Catalan Education Department temporarily downgraded the C2 Catalan-language certificate from a mandatory requirement to a merit for joining the substitute teacher pool in 2026-27, citing an “structural insufficiency” of accredited staff that could leave classrooms uncovered, so new hires must instead pledge to obtain the proficiency by the end of next school year while those already certified retain priority in job assignments.
stories.headline: Catalonia delays C2 Catalan requirement for new teachers amid staffing shortages
